page: Amazon Web Services i8g.2xlarge is a storage-optimized server instance powered by custom AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processors. Built on the 64-bit ARM architecture, this instance features 8 dedicated physical cores running at 2.8 GHz with a single thread per core. The CPU cache configuration includes 64 KB L1 data cache, 2 MB L2 cache, and 36 MB L3 cache. It is equipped with 64.0 GB of system memory, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core, and includes a bundled 1875 GB NVMe SSD for high-speed local storage. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 5 Gbps.

In benchmark evaluations, the i8g.2xlarge demonstrates strong memory performance, achieving a top-tier Passmark memory score in the top 10% of tested systems. Memory bandwidth tests show read-only speeds of 924.57 GB/sec for small cached blocks and 154.96 GB/sec for large uncached blocks, alongside a write speed of 83.0 GB/sec. Single-threaded bzip3 decompression and Geekbench text processing both rank in the top 25%. However, CPU encryption performance is relatively weak, scoring in the bottom 25% on the Passmark encryption test. Additionally, medium-model LLM prompt processing is slow, registering in the bottom 10% at 15.47 tokens per second.

From a resource density perspective, the i8g.2xlarge offers a highly efficient balance of dedicated ARM64 processing cores, a high memory-to-vCPU ratio, and substantial local NVMe SSD storage. This combination makes it well-suited for storage-intensive and database workloads, such as Redis deployments, which achieved 17.53 million operations per second in testing. It is also highly capable for static web serving, text processing, and memory-bound applications that do not rely heavily on x86-specific instruction sets or intensive cryptographic acceleration.
description: The Amazon Web Services i8g.2xlarge is an ARM64 storage-optimized server instance powered by an 8-core AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processor running at 2.8 GHz. It features 64.0 GB of memory and a bundled 1875 GB NVMe SSD for high-speed local storage, managed via the Nitro hypervisor. Benchmark results highlight top-tier memory performance, with a Passmark memory score in the top 10% and cached read bandwidth reaching 924.57 GB/sec. Single-threaded bzip3 decompression and text processing also perform strongly in the top 25%. However, cryptographic encryption and medium-model LLM prompt processing show lower relative performance. This instance is highly efficient for storage-intensive applications, Redis databases, static web serving, and memory-bound workloads requiring dedicated physical cores and fast local storage.
og_description: Amazon Web Services i8g.2xlarge is an ARM64 storage-optimized server featuring 8 Graviton Neoverse-V2 cores, 64 GB RAM, and a 1875 GB local NVMe SSD, delivering top-tier memory performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services i8g.2xlarge is an ARM64 storage-optimized server with 8 Graviton cores, 64 GB RAM, and a 1875 GB NVMe SSD.
tagline: An ARM64 storage-optimized platform combining dedicated physical cores, high-speed local NVMe SSD storage, and top-tier memory bandwidth.
bullet_points: 8 dedicated AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 ARM64 cores running at 2.8 GHz
64.0 GB of system memory paired with a 1875 GB local NVMe SSD
Top-tier memory performance ranking in the top 10% of benchmarks
Strong single-threaded decompression and text processing capabilities
Optimized for local databases, Redis, and static web serving
Baseline network bandwidth of 5 Gbps managed by the Nitro hypervisor
